response.justification: Bogdan brings rare geospatial domain expertise and proven distributed-systems thinking from shipping high-throughput microservices at scale, plus a demonstrated ability to rapidly acquire technical competencies across languages and domains. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ as the primary skill, and his last substantial C++ work was in 2014-2015; the past decade has centered on TypeScript and higher-level abstractions. His geospatial experience is primarily in web-based GIS applications and data collection platforms rather than routing tile pipelines or optimization systems. Interview if your team can support a 2-3 month ramp-up on modern C++ and routing-specific challenges; otherwise, prioritize candidates with recent, production-grade C++ experience.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise with shipped applications (GIS platforms, open-source geo-data systems) directly relevant to navigation and mapping data
Proven distributed-systems thinking: handled millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases for real-time metadata
Exceptional learning agility: shipped production systems across C++, DLang, TypeScript, Swift, and Java—demonstrates ability to rapidly acquire new technical domains
concerns: C++ experience is stale (last substantial work: 2014-2015 at National Instruments); past decade dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and higher-level abstractions—would require significant ramp-up on modern C++17+, STL, and performance patterns
Navigation routing pipeline experience absent; geospatial background is in data collection and web applications rather than tile generation, routing optimization, or real-time traffic integration
No Rust experience listed, despite job offering it as primary alternative—only evidence of ability to learn systems languages through past C++ work, which is now dated

key_factors.factor: C++ recency and modern toolchain gap
